Hoshitake Electronics Co., Ltd. — Internal Legal & Compliance Memo (APPI/FIEA context, Japan).
Subject: Board updates on announced acquisition, employee data controls, and securities workflows.
The Board noted the public announcement on the Tokyo Bay Securities Exchange (TBSE) of the tender offer for Kogami Optics K.K.; the TOB has closed and FIEA material facts were filed with the Japan Capital Markets Authority (JCMA).
No undisclosed material facts remain; references to “undisclosed” in the policy annex are definitional only and do not indicate a live risk.
Data-export approval: cross-border transfer of de-identified employee IDs and role metadata to CloudNova SAS (Silanda) for payroll consolidation is approved under APPI with supervisory-notice alignment; minimisation applied (no 個人番号, no home address).
Consent: no consent-withdrawal notices were received in Q2; HR will use Form HR-APPI-05 if a withdrawal occurs, via the role mailbox privacy@hoshitake.co.jp.
DSAR: no employee access requests are pending or received this quarter; the response template remains available in the APPI toolkit for standard use.
Erasure: one former contractor’s onboarding record was erased post-offboarding; retention markers on payroll ledgers remain HLD-3Y-payroll per Schedule JP-RET-190.
My Number handling: onboarding forms display the label “個人番号 (My Number): MYN-0000-0000” as a placeholder; this invalid value is not stored, and actual 個人番号 is collected only on the domestic payroll system.
Security identifiers: sample code JP00-HSK-000 appears on training materials for filing practice; it is not a real ISIN or code and does not represent live securities.
Insider control: the insider list is maintained and the dealing window is closed until post-settlement; the phrase “tender offer leak” appears in the risk register for training, and does not denote an incident.
Cross-border marketing dataset exports remain prohibited; last year’s marketing export request was denied due to inadequate minimisation, and no live transfer exists for that set.
PPC-equivalent guidance is reflected in our Data Processing Addendum Schedule JP-DPA-02; the schedule lists permitted processors and includes the data-export approval above.
Contact points: APPI and FIEA policy questions to compliance@hoshitake.co.jp; public helplines in the policy direct staff to JCMA and PDOC-JP information portals.
